caseOutcomeOptions.label: Diversion, deferred disposition, supervision, or similar program

pathways.label: Conviction or diversion expungement under K.S.A. 21-6614
pathways.summary: The petition identifies whether relief is requested for a conviction or diversion and includes arrest, charge, court, disposition, and final-discharge facts. The form provides offense-dependent one-, three-, five-, or ten-year periods after diversion completion, sentence satisfaction, or discharge from supervision. The person must not have been convicted of a felony in the prior two years and no felony proceeding may be pending or being instituted. The offense cannot be one of the…
ruleClauses: The petition identifies whether relief is requested for a conviction or diversion and includes arrest, charge, court, disposition, and final-discharge facts.
The form provides offense-dependent one-, three-, five-, or ten-year periods after diversion completion, sentence satisfaction, or discharge from supervision.
The person must not have been convicted of a felony in the prior two years and no felony proceeding may be pending or being instituted.
The offense cannot be one of the no-expungement offenses listed in K.S.A. 21-6614(e).
A person required to register as a sex or violent offender cannot receive expungement while registration is required.
The petition is filed in the county of arrest, followed by notice, a hearing, and proposed grant/denial orders.
waitingRules: The form provides offense-dependent one-, three-, five-, or ten-year periods after diversion completion, sentence satisfaction, or discharge from supervision.
The person must not have been convicted of a felony in the prior two years and no felony proceeding may be pending or being instituted.
exclusionRules: The offense cannot be one of the no-expungement offenses listed in K.S.A. 21-6614(e).
A person required to register as a sex or violent offender cannot receive expungement while registration is required.

frontendBranch.entryCondition: Backend matched this jurisdiction-specific route after evaluating the cited source rules.

pathways.label: Specialty-court completion route
pathways.summary: The form provides a route for a person who successfully completed a specialty court program when the conviction would ordinarily be eligible after three years. No felony proceeding may be pending or being instituted. The backend must confirm the offense is otherwise eligible before offering the petition packet.
ruleClauses: The form provides a route for a person who successfully completed a specialty court program when the conviction would ordinarily be eligible after three years.
No felony proceeding may be pending or being instituted.
The backend must confirm the offense is otherwise eligible before offering the petition packet.
waitingRules: The form provides a route for a person who successfully completed a specialty court program when the conviction would ordinarily be eligible after three years.

frontendBranch.entryCondition: Backend matched this jurisdiction-specific route after evaluating the cited source rules.

pathways.label: Prostitution offense committed under coercion
pathways.summary: The petition includes a specific coercion checkbox for a prostitution conviction or diversion. The source defines coercion through threats, restraint, schemes creating fear of harm, or abuse/threatened abuse of legal process. This route remains subject to the applicable statutory timing and filing process.
ruleClauses: The petition includes a specific coercion checkbox for a prostitution conviction or diversion.
The source defines coercion through threats, restraint, schemes creating fear of harm, or abuse/threatened abuse of legal process.
This route remains subject to the applicable statutory timing and filing process.

pathways.label: Drug-offender registration relief coordinated with expungement
pathways.summary: The source says a person registered as a drug offender may be able to seek registration relief and expungement together if all requirements are met. The frontend must treat this as a coordinated special route and not apply the sex/violent-offender bar automatically.
ruleClauses: The source says a person registered as a drug offender may be able to seek registration relief and expungement together if all requirements are met.
The frontend must treat this as a coordinated special route and not apply the sex/violent-offender bar automatically.

generatorSelectionContract.failureBehavior: Return needs_more_info or needs_review. Never fall back to a generic packet or another jurisdiction generator.

packet_ready.title: You may have a packet-ready path.

packet_ready_with_caution.title: You may have a path, with cautions.

needs_more_info.title: We need a few more details.

guidance_only.title: We can give you state-specific next steps.

not_covered_yet.title: This record type is not supported by the current packet set.

likely_not_eligible.title: This record may not match a self-help filing path.

needs_review.title: This situation needs review before a packet is generated.

hard_stop.title: This tool cannot prepare a packet for this matter.

copyGuardrails: Use Kansas-specific remedy terminology returned by the profile; do not relabel every remedy as expungement.
Use “may qualify,” “possible path,” and “the court or agency makes the final decision.”
Do not promise erasure, sealing, eligibility, timing, filing acceptance, or a court outcome.
Do not show checkout unless the backend returns a packet-ready result and paymentAllowed=true.

sourceSections.title: Complete the Petition for Expungement of Conviction or Diversion. Where your name is
sourceSections.text: required, insert your full legal name.

sourceSections.title: Sign the Petition for Expungement of Conviction or Diversion. Your signature is your
sourceSections.text:
affirmation that all of the information in the petition is accurate to the best of your
knowledge.

4. File the original petition and required number of copies with the Clerk of the Court in the
county in which you were arrested and pay the required docket fee. (You will need to check
with the Clerk to find out how many copies are required.)

sourceSections.title: If you do not know the case number of the matter for which you are seeking expungement, you
sourceSections.text:
may obtain the case number from the Clerk of the Court when you file.

6. Contact the Clerk of the Court or the Administrative Assistant of the judge to which the case
has been assigned, and ask for a date and time for a hearing before the court. Ask for the
division or room number of the hearing as well.

7. Complete the Notice of Hearing and make 2 additional copies. File the original notice and
copies with the Clerk of the Court who will send a copy to the office of the attorney who
prosecuted your case and the arresting law enforcement agency.

sourceSections.title: Complete paragraphs numbered 1 through 4 of the Order for Expungement of Conviction or
sourceSections.text:
Diversion and paragraphs numbered 1 through 4 of the Order Denying Expungement of
Conviction or Diversion (just in case).

sourceSections.title: Bring Order of Expungement of Conviction or Diversion Cover Sheet, Order for
sourceSections.text:
Expungement of Conviction or Diversion, and Order Denying Expungement of Conviction or
Diversion to the hearing.

11. Attend the hearing and answer any questions the court may have.

===== SOURCE FILE: Petition for Expungement of Conviction or Diversion 82022.pdf =====

IN THE JUDICIAL DISTRICT
DISTRICT COURT OF COUNTY, KANSAS

THE STATE OF KANSAS
Case No. _____________
vs.

_______________________________ [Name]
Defendant

PETITION FOR EXPUNGEMENT OF CONVICTION OR DIVERSION
Pursuant to K.S.A. 21-6614.

I respectfully request of the Court an order of expungement of my conviction and

related arrest records OR diversion record and related arrest records. In support thereof, I state

the following:

1. My full name is __________________________________________________.

sourceSections.title: My full name at the time of my arrest or conviction, if different than #1, was
The conviction or diversion for which expungement is sought is not one of the offenses set
sourceSections.text:
out in K.S.A. 21-6614(e), for which no expungement is permitted.

Rev. KSJC 08/2022 2

I affirm under penalty of perjury that the statements in this Petition are accurate to the best

of my knowledge and I respectfully request that the Court set this matter for hearing and grant the

expungement.

_________________________________________
Defendant, Pro Se
Name (Print):______________________________
Address 1: ________________________________
Address 2: ________________________________
City, State, Zip: ____________________________
Telephone Number: _________________________
Fax Number: _____________________________
E-mail Address:___________________________

Rev. KSJC 08/2022 3

Authority

K.S.A. 21-6614.

Notes on Use

A person may not receive an expungement for convictions of:
• Rape • Voluntary manslaughter
• Indecent liberties with a child • Involuntary manslaughter
• Aggravated indecent liberties with a • Involuntary manslaughter while
child driving under the influence
• Criminal sodomy • Sexual battery when the victim is less
• Aggravated criminal sodomy than 18 years of age
• Indecent solicitation of a child • Aggravated sexual battery
• Aggravated indecent solicitation of a • Commercial driving under the
child influence, including any diversion
• Sexual exploitation of a child for such violation
• Internet trading in child pornography • Any comparable offense if the
• Aggravated internet trading in child conviction was in effect at any time
pornography prior to July 1, 2011.
• Aggravated incest
• Endangering a child K.S.A. 21-6614(e).
• Aggravated endangering a child
• Abuse of a child
• Capital murder
• Murder in the first degree
• Murder in the second degree

A person who is required to register as a sex offender or violent offender under the Kansas
Offender Registration Act may not receive an expungement for any conviction while the person
is required to register. However, a person who is required to register as a drug offender under
the Kansas Offender Registration Act may be eligible to petition for relief from registration and
for expungement of the drug offense at the same time if all applicable requirements are met. See
the chart on the next page for the applicable time requirements for expungement of drug
offenses. Forms to request relief from drug offender registration are also available on the
Judicial Council website.

Please consult the chart on the next page if your crime is
not listed above.

Rev. KSJC 08/2022 4

July 1, 1993

* Based on State v. Anderson, 12 Kan. App. 2d 342, 744 P.2d 143 (1987), the law in effect at the time of the offense
applies with regard to eligibility for expungement unless the current law is more beneficial to the defendant. The
current statute, K.S.A. 21-6614(d)(2), says a second or subsequent DUI conviction under K.S.A. 8-1567 cannot be
expunged until 10 or more years have elapsed, but from July 1, 2014, to June 30, 2015, the statute allowed any DUI
conviction or diversion to be expunged after 7 years. L. 2014 Ch. 102, § 6, effective July 1, 2014; L. 2015 Ch. 71, §
4, effective July 1, 2015. For a first DUI conviction or diversion, the current law, allowing expungement after 5 years,
applies because it is more beneficial to a defendant than the 7 years required from July 1, 2014, to June 30, 2015. For
a second or subsequent DUI conviction, the current statute, K.S.A. 21-6614(d)(2), does not allow expungement until
10 or more years have elapsed. Therefore, for second or subsequent DUIs that were committed on July 1, 2014,
through June 30, 2015, the statute in effect at the time of the crime that allowed expungement after 7 years applies
because it is more beneficial to the defendant.
+
Completion of a Specialty Court Program
A person who has completed the requirements of a specialty court program may petition for immediate expungement
of any offense which is otherwise eligible for expungement after 3 years. This early expungement opportunity does
not apply to offenses for which expungement is not available or to offenses which require a 1, 5, 7 or 10-year waiting
period.

Rev. KSJC 08/2022 6
